channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US Conducts Strikes Against Iranian Targets in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States has intensified military operations against Iranian forces in the Strait of Hormuz, aiming to disrupt their ability to threaten international shipping. US Central Command confirmed a...

item.title: Bishnoi Gang's Criminal Activities Pose National Security Risk in Canada
item.description: The article highlights escalating concerns over the Bishnoi gang's criminal activities in Canada, which have expanded through lax immigration laws. Experts warn that the group's actions—linked to kill...

item.title: Long-term Health Impacts and Costs for Retired Elite Athletes
item.description: The article explores the ongoing health challenges faced by retired elite athletes, using former All Black Sonny Bill Williams as a case study. Williams, preparing for his fourth neck surgery, highlig...

item.title: Quebec Court Rules Mandatory Minimum Sentence for Child Sex Abuse Material Unconstitutional Due to Defendant’s Mental Health
item.description: A Quebec court has ruled that the mandatory one-year minimum sentence for distributing child sexual abuse material is unconstitutional, citing the defendant's intellectual disability and mental health...

item.title: Ekurhuleni officials face arrest over corruption allegations
item.description: Former Ekurhuleni city manager Imogen Mashazi has been instructed to present herself to police as part of an investigation into corruption and malfeasance within the municipality. This follows the arr...

item.title: Three-year-old boy undergoes six surgeries after horrific zoo incident
item.description: A three-year-old child remains hospitalized following a traumatic incident at Johnsons of Old Hurst zoo in Huntingdon, England. The boy was allegedly thrown into a crocodile enclosure by an unknown ma...

item.title: Three men get life sentences for murdering 18-year-old during schoolyard robbery
item.description: Three men have been handed life imprisonment for the murder of Lerato Nchabeleng, an 18-year-old Grade 11 learner who was stabbed to death outside his school in Protea North, Soweto. The Protea region...

item.title: BBC 6 Music launches 'Indie Forever' stream celebrating UK indie music history
item.description: BBC Radio 6 Music has unveiled its new BBC Sounds extension 'Indie Forever', a 24-hour stream dedicated to indie pop, rock, and classics from the 1980s to the 2010s. Launching on July 22, the stream w...

item.title: South African court ruling challenges Canada's rhino horn import ban
item.description: A South African High Court has dismissed the government's appeal against the export of over 500 rhino horns, potentially testing the adequacy of Canada's ban on raw rhino horn imports. The case involv...

item.title: Presidency dismisses Obi’s claims of government targeting amid political tensions
item.description: The Nigerian presidency has strongly denied allegations by former Anambra governor Peter Obi that he is being targeted by the federal government. Special Adviser Bayo Onanuga dismissed Obi's recent cl...

item.title: Centre Announces Major Upgrades for Smarter Security and Faster Immigration at Indian Airports
item.description: The Union Government of India has taken significant steps to improve airport facilities across the country. During a high-level review meeting chaired by Union Home Minister Amit Shah along with Civil...

item.title: DFA Investigates Harassment Claims Against Claire Castro During Canada Trip
item.description: The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) is investigating allegations of harassment faced by Claire Castro, a Palace Press Officer, during President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.'s official visit to Vancouver, ...

item.title: Kenyan Universities Offer Financial Aid to Students
item.description: The article highlights several Kenyan universities that provide financial aid programs to support needy and academically bright students. Zetech University offers scholarships for athletes and law stu...

item.title: Tazara Revitalization Honors Railway Workers and Promotes Regional Development
item.description: The Tanzania-Zambia Railway Authority (Tazara) commemorated its 50th anniversary with memorial ceremonies honoring Chinese experts and workers who died during the railway's construction. The event, th...

item.title: NGO Executive Convicted for Misusing P2.4M PDAF Funds
item.description: The Sandiganbayan Fifth Division has convicted Petronila Balmaceda, president of Pangkabuhayan Foundation, Inc. (PFI), of malversation for misusing P2.4 million Priority Development Assistance Fund (P...

item.title: Radie Peat Explores 'Two Extremes' of Love in Haunting Solo Single
item.description: Radie Peat, best known as a member of the Irish band Lankum, has released her debut solo single 'Still I Love Him', reworking a traditional love song with close collaborator John Francis Flynn. The tr...

item.title: Sirena Linton reflects on her surprise WWE release and shares her reaction
item.description: Former WWE talent Sirena Linton has spoken publicly about her unexpected release from the company, describing the moment she received the phone call informing her that her contract had been terminated...

item.title: NATO Summit Highlights Both Unity and Tensions Amid Alliance Discussions
item.description: US President Donald Trump has asserted there was 'tremendous unity' at the NATO summit in Turkey, despite underlying tensions within the alliance. During the meeting, Trump emphasized his commitment t...

item.title: Former Minister Alleges N200m Bribe Demand During Budget Defence
item.description: Former Minister of Youth and Sports Development, Solomon Dalung, accused lawmakers of demanding a N200 million bribe during his first budget presentation under President Muhammadu Buhari. He claimed t...

item.title: Systemic Failures in KZN Healthcare Highlighted in Ombud Report
item.description: The health ombud's investigation into the deaths of six healthcare workers in KwaZulu-Natal revealed severe systemic issues across public hospitals. Key findings include chronic staff shortages due to...
